task.name: Implement statutory deadline timestamp verification
task.description: Add logic to the comp-statutory-deadline-verifier that checks a work order's completion timestamp against its statutory deadline, flags overdue status, and emits an event if exceeded.

completion_criteria.description: The verifier correctly identifies when a work order’s completion timestamp meets the statutory deadline and emits an alert if exceeded.

user_stories.action: ensure statutory deadline compliance for job completion reporting
user_stories.outcome: Job status updated to indicate whether the completion timestamp meets the statutory deadline; if exceeded, a DEADLINE_EXCEEDED event is emitted and a notification is sent to the HOA board when applicable. An audit log entry is created for each verification.

acceptance_criteria.description: Job completion timestamp is within the statutory deadline.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: if (completion_timestamp - work_order_creation_timestamp) <= statutory_deadline_days then status == 'deadline_met' and no alert event generated

acceptance_criteria.description: Happy path – job completed before or on the statutory deadline.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: if (completion_timestamp - work_order_creation_timestamp) <= statutory_deadline_days then status == 'completed_within_deadline' and DEADLINE_EXCEEDED alert not emitted

acceptance_criteria.description: Deadline exceeded triggers an alert and HOA notification if applicable.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: if (completion_timestamp - work_order_creation_timestamp) > statutory_deadline_days then DEADLINE_EXCEEDED event emitted and notification sent to HOA board when property is under HOA jurisdiction

acceptance_criteria.description: Missing completion timestamp results in an error during verification.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: if completion_timestamp is null then system returns HTTP 400 with JSON {"error_code": "MISSING_COMPLETION_TIMESTAMP"}

acceptance_criteria.description: Audit log entry created for each deadline verification.
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: for every verification performed a new audit_log record is written containing job_id, work_order_creation_timestamp, completion_timestamp, statutory_deadline_days, and outcome ('deadline_met' or 'deadline_exceeded')

nfrs.description: Job completion timestamps must be verified against statutory deadline days.
nfrs.threshold: All job completion timestamps must be within the statutory deadline period defined by COMP-STATUTORY-DEADLINES; any timestamp outside this period violates the NFR.
nfrs.measurement_method: Automated compliance checker runs nightly against job completion logs, comparing each timestamp to the statutory deadline days and flagging any violations for immediate remediation.

responsibilities.description: Retrieve completed job records from Work Order Service.
Compare completion timestamps against statutory deadlines and flag overdue jobs within five minutes.

evaluation_criteria.evaluation_method: Generate a set of random job completions with realistic timestamps, compute the difference between completion_timestamp and work_order_creation_timestamp, and assert that each difference is less than or equal to the statutory deadline days. Verify the invariant for all generated instances.
evaluation_criteria.success_condition: All job completion timestamps must be within the statutory deadline period defined by COMP-STATUTORY-DEADLINES; any timestamp outside this period violates the NFR.

property_spec.invariant: for every job completion event, the difference between completion_timestamp and work_order_creation_timestamp <= statutory_deadline_days defined by COMP-STATUTORY-DEADLINES
property_spec.input_domain: job completions with random timestamps within realistic ranges

active_constraints.text: Node.js (Bun) | Bun for high-performance API execution.
oRPC | A function-based API layer using Zod for strict type safety and automatic OpenAPI/SDK generation.

task.name: Retrieve completed job records for deadline verification
task.description: Open the repository layer, query WorkOrderService for jobs with status 'Completed', and return the list of job IDs and completion timestamps.

completion_criteria.description: Service retrieves all completed work orders with status 'Completed' and includes their completion timestamps.

task.name: Enforce job completion before invoice creation
task.description: Add a check to the POST /invoices endpoint that queries the referenced work order status; if it is not 'completed', reject the request with HTTP 409 and do not create an invoice or ledger entry.

completion_criteria.description: Invoice submission fails with HTTP 409 when referenced work order status is not 'completed'.

user_stories.outcome: Invoice recorded and linked to work order

acceptance_criteria.description: Invoice must be linked to an existing work order (POST /invoices with a valid work_order_id returns 201 and GET /work_orders/{id} within 1 second returns the work order record that includes this invoice id in its invoices collection)
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: POST /invoices with valid work_order_id returns HTTP 201; subsequent GET /work_orders/{id} within 1000 ms returns a JSON object whose invoices array contains the new invoice id

acceptance_criteria.description: Invoice creation requires mandatory fields (POST /invoices missing any required field (amount, vendor_id, due_date) returns HTTP 400 with error code MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD and no record is persisted)
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: POST /invoices without amount/vendor_id/due_date returns HTTP status 400; response body contains error_code 'MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD'; database query for the new invoice id returns zero rows

acceptance_criteria.description: Invoice stored successfully (POST /invoices returns 201 and GET /invoices/{id} returns the same data within 1 second)
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: POST /invoices returns HTTP 201; subsequent GET /invoices/{new_id} within 1000 ms returns a JSON object identical to the payload sent in POST

acceptance_criteria.description: Invoice cannot be submitted if the associated job is not marked complete (Attempting POST /invoices when the referenced work order status is not 'completed' returns HTTP 409 with error code JOB_NOT_COMPLETE and no invoice record is created)
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: POST /invoices to a work_order whose status != 'completed' returns HTTP 409; response body contains error_code 'JOB_NOT_COMPLETE'; database query for the new invoice id returns zero rows

acceptance_criteria.description: Audit trail entry is created upon successful invoice submission (After POST /invoices succeeds, an audit log entry exists with actor_id equal to the service provider id, action='invoice.create', reference_id matching the new invoice id, and timestamp within 2 seconds of request completion)
acceptance_criteria.measurable_condition: Immediately after a POST /invoices returns HTTP 201, query audit_log where reference_id = new_invoice_id AND action = 'invoice.create' AND actor_id = service_provider_id; ensure record exists and timestamp <= now() + interval '2 second'

responsibilities.description: Link invoices to corresponding work orders

preconditions: A work order with ID 42 exists and its status is 'completed'.
The user has permission to create invoices for the given work order.
test_cases.expected_outcome: POST /invoices returns HTTP 201, the returned JSON contains an invoice_id that matches the one stored in the database. A subsequent GET /invoices/{invoice_id} returns exactly the same payload as sent.

preconditions: A work order with ID 42 exists and its status is 'completed'.
test_cases.expected_outcome: POST /invoices with any required field omitted (amount, vendor_id or due_date) returns HTTP 400. The response body contains error code MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD and no invoice record is persisted.

preconditions: A work order with ID 42 exists but its status is 'in_progress'.
test_cases.expected_outcome: POST /invoices attempts to create an invoice for a non-completed job and returns HTTP 409. The response body contains error code JOB_NOT_COMPLETE, and no invoice record is created.

preconditions: A work order with ID 42 exists and its status is 'completed'.
The actor performing the request has a valid service provider ID.
test_cases.expected_outcome: After a successful POST /invoices, an audit log entry is persisted within 2 seconds. The entry contains actor_id equal to the service provider’s ID, action='invoice.create', reference_id matching the new invoice id, and a timestamp that falls within two seconds of request completion.

preconditions: A work order with ID 42 exists and its status is 'completed'.
test_cases.expected_outcome: Property holds for every generated input: creating an invoice and subsequently retrieving it yields the same data.

property_spec.input_domain: syntactically valid JSON payloads containing required fields (amount, vendor_id, due_date) with arbitrary numeric and string values within reasonable ranges.
property_spec.oracle: identity of the original input payload
